They said one to another, It is manna: for they wist not what it was.— This is a most unfortunate translation, because it not only gives no sense, but it contradicts itself. The Hebrew man hu, literally signifies, What is this? for, says the text, they wist not what it was, and therefore they could not give it a name. Moses immediately answers the question, and says, This is the bread which the…

Let no man leave of it till the morning.— For God would have them to take no thought for the morrow, and constantly to depend on him for their dally bread. And is not that petition in our Lord’s prayer founded on this very circumstance, Give us day by day our daily bread? (Adam Clarke, Bible Commentary - Volume 1)

REMEMBER THE SABBATH DAY, TO SANCTIFY IT. Said R. Isaac: ‘It is written, “And God blessed the seventh day” (Gen. 2, 3); and yet we read of the manna, “Six days ye shall gather it, but on the seventh day, the Sabbath, in it there shall be none” (Ex. 16, 26). If there was no food on that day what blessing is attached to it?

matches.lb: Midrash: Ein Yaakov (Glick Edition), Bava Kama 8:4
matches.x: Said Raba to Rabba bar Mari: "Whence do we deduce the following people's saying: With the shrub the cabbage is smitten (the good suffer with the bad)?" He answered: "From the following verse (Jer. 2, 29) Wherefore will ye contend with me? Ye all (including the righteous) have transgressed against Me, saith the Lord." "You deduce it from this verse," said he to him, "but I deduce it from the…

And the Lord had finished by the Seventh Day the work which He had wrought, and the ten formations which He had created between the suns; and He rested the Seventh Day from all His works which He had performed.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Covenant and Conversation; Leviticus; The Book of Holiness, Leviticus; The Democratisation of Holiness 69
matches.x: Thus the heavens and the earth, and all the host of them, were finished. And on the seventh day God ended His work which He had done, and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had done. Then God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because in it He rested from all His work which God had created and made. (Gen. 2:1–3)

matches.lb: Liturgy: Siddur Sefard, Holidays, Yom Tov Eve Kiddush 6
matches.x: The sixth day.  And the heavens and earth were finished and all that they contain.  And God finished on the seventh day the work that God had done.  And God rested on the seventh day from all the work that God had done.  And God blessed the seventh day and made it holy.  For on that day God rested from all the work the work that God created.

matches.lb: Liturgy: Siddur Edot HaMizrach, Shabbat Evening, Kiddush 9
matches.x: The Sixth Day: And they were completed - the Heavens & the Earth & all their Hosts. And G·d finished, on the Seventh Day, all the work that He had done & He rested on the Seventh Day from all the work that He had done. And G·d Blessed the Seventh Day & sanctified it, for on it He rested from all His work – that G·d created to make.

And G·d finished, on the Seventh Day, all the work that He had done & He rested on the Seventh Day from all the work that He had done. And G·d Blessed the Seventh Day & sanctified it, for on it He rested from all His work – that G·d created to make.

CHAPTER XVI. Quails and manna sent. ND they took their journey from Hlim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month . after their departing out of the land of Egypt.

The omer as a measure never recurs in the Bible. The note is needed here because the omer became obsolete and unintelligible to later generations. A tenth of an ephah is otherwise termed issaron. The ephah, a word of Egyptian origin, was a dry measure frequently mentioned in the Bible. It seems to have been the name of a vessel, a meaning it still has in Zechariah 5:6-10, where it is large enough…
